results.title: Toyota adds another $1.5B to its bet on startups at every stage - TechCrunch
results.content: The Japanese automaker said Tuesday it has created a strategic investment subsidiary called Toyota Invention Partners Co. with about $670 million in capital, while its growth-stage venture arm Woven Capital launched a second $800 million fund. “One way to think about them (Toyota Invention Partners) is they’re bookending what Toyota Ventures and Woven Capital are doing,” Kellerman said in a phone interview. Machina Labs, a Los Angeles-based advanced manufacturing startup that combines AI and robotics to rapidly produce metal structures, announced a strategic investment from Woven Capital and a pilot project with Toyota Motor North America. Woven Capital, which launched in 2021 with an $800 million fund, is still focused on investing in global startups that are entering into a growth stage.

results.title: Toyota Fuels US Workforce With $14 Billion Battery Plant - Benzinga
results.content: Best Stocks & ETFs Best Swing Trade Stocks Best Stocks to Day Trade My Stocks Calendars Analyst Ratings Calendar Stock Split Calendar **Toyota Motor Corporation** (NYSE:TM) marked a major milestone on Wednesday as it began producing batteries at its new Liberty, North Carolina facility. The combined spending brings Toyota's total U.S. investment to nearly $60 billion since it entered the market almost seven decades ago. "Today's launch of Toyota's first U.S. battery plant and additional U.S. investment up to $10 billion marks a pivotal moment in our company's history," said **Ted Ogawa**, chief executive of Toyota Motor North America. results.title: Toyota takes Tier IV stake, MoU in regional autonomy push - Automotive World
results.content: Toyota takes Tier IV stake, MoU in regional autonomy push Einride goes public on Nasdaq with US$1.35bn valuation Sonatus edge AI embeds into Schaeffler SDV control units GM unveils V2G, sodium-ion and second-life battery plans Xos secures US$3m Hub order from autonomous fleet operator AT&T Intelligent Transportation Platform adds Derq AI data Uber and Wayve prepare London's first robotaxi service Mexico unveils state-backed EV programme, debuts model US DoD adds BYD, Nio and CATL to Chinese army blacklist Mercedes-Benz starts axial flux motor production in Berlin. Home › News › Toyota takes Tier IV stake, MoU in regional autonomy push. # Toyota takes Tier IV stake, MoU in regional autonomy push. Toyota has taken a 1% stake in Japanese autonomous driving startup Tier IV through its subsidiary Toyota Invention Partners, acquiring the interest for approximately JP¥1bn (US$6.2m) alongside a memorandum of understanding on technology collaboration. Under the MoU, the two companies will collaborate on the development and deployment of self-driving systems, with Tier IV providing its Autoware-based software stack and Toyota contributing its vehicle platforms and manufacturing expertise.

results.title: Toyota's buyout deal is a bigger win for Elliott than for governance - Reuters
results.content: TOKYO, March 3 (Reuters) - Toyota's (7203.T), opens new tab decision to further sweeten its bid for group company Toyota Industries (6201.T), opens new tab marks a win for activist fund Elliott Investment Management, ​which had pushed the automaker for months for a heftier bump up in price. The Toyota group originally offered 16,300 yen a share in June, sparking outrage from minority shareholders who said the deal was underpriced and lacked transparency. They cited inadequate financial ​disclosure ‍and said Toyota group companies should not be classified as minority shareholders, as that lowers the voting threshold Toyota would need to clinch the deal. While the outcome was a "big improvement" in terms of governance in Japan compared to 10 or even five years ago, there were still "many weak points" in ​the deal that limited the benefit for ​minority shareholders, the investor said.

results.title: Toyota reaffirms auto industry growth commitment - Daily Tribune
results.content: # Toyota reaffirms auto industry growth commitment. ## ‘Across infrastructure, manufacturing, energy, mobility, logistics, digital transformation, trade, tourism, and services, Japanese enterprise has become deeply embedded in the Philippine development journey.’. TOKYO — Japanese automotive giant Toyota Motor Corporation (TMC) reaffirmed its commitment to the Philippines, pledging continued support for the country’s automotive manufacturing sector and broader industrial ecosystem. During the high-level Tokyo Business Roundtable on Thursday, held as part of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.’s state visit to Japan, TMC Asia Region CEO Masahiko Maeda highlighted Toyota’s nearly 38 years of operations in the Philippines and its role in supporting local economic growth. Also read:Toyota reaffirms commitment to Philippine auto industry growth. For his part, President Marcos underscored the long-term value of Japanese investments, describing Japanese companies as key contributors to the country’s development. Across infrastructure, manufacturing, energy, mobility, logistics, digital transformation, trade, tourism, and services, Japanese enterprise has become deeply embedded in the Philippine development journey,” Marcos told Japanese business leaders.

results.title: Toyota to Invest $10 Billion in US Manufacturing and EV Infrastructure - Букви
results.content: Categories:  News # Toyota to Invest $10 Billion in US Manufacturing and EV Infrastructure These plans confirm the company’s aim to expand production capabilities in the U.S. and support the development of infrastructure for future electric vehicles. The CEO of Toyota Motor North America, Tetsuo Ogawa, voiced these statements during the opening of a new battery production plant in Liberty, North Carolina. He called this move a “turning point” in the company’s history and stressed that the investments will help create more jobs across the United States and reflect the long-standing commitment to the principle “build where we sell.” * News * News * News * News * News * News
